Final Approval Hearing
Off Calendar Oct-15-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Scott E. Cole, Esq. and Andrew Weaver, Esq. of Scott Cole & Associates, T:510.891.9800, for plaintiff and moving party;
Arin Norijanian, Esq. of Bradley & Gmelich, LLP, T:818.243.5200, for defendant, on Court Call.

RULING: Hearing held. The Settlement Administrator was successful in contacting almost all of the class members. Therefore, there being no objection by any party, and to the satisfaction of the Court, the Motion For An Order (1)Granting Approval of the Class Action Settlement Agreement, (2)Awarding Attorneys' Fees And Costs To Class Counsel, (3)Awarding Enhancement To The Representative Plaintiff, and (4)Awarding Reimbursement Of Settlement Administration Fees And Costs is granted.

Compliance hearing is set for January 5, 2022 at 9:30am in Department 302.

Order signed in open court. [See order for details.]

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

Andrew D. Weaver, esq. (925-355-5200) of Scott Cole & Associates for Plaintiff.
Arin Norijanian, esq. (818-243-5200) of Bradley & Gmelich for Defendant Corporate Security Service, Inc., et al via Court Call.

RULING - A R G U E D. Hearing is held; the Court notes it does not have documents to review in camera as expected. Mr. Norijanian stated they reconsidered providing documents and the matter can go forward without the documents. Mr. Weaver stated they understood the documents would be produced today and hoped defendant would change their position. Mr. Norijanian listed documents it can produce, and the Court added which ones would be relevant to the matter before the court (as fully reported in the reporter's notes). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Plaintiff Laverne Harbor'S Motion For Preliminary Approval Of Class Action Settlement - (Specially Set At 9:00 Am Before Judge Kahn)

Continued to April 25, 2019 at 9:00am in Dept 302, Law and Motion. Documents in support of counsel's positions to be filed, served and courtesy copies delivered to Dept. 302 by 4/19/19 (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript).
